title: notholaena marantae subsp. subcordata
intro: Notholaena marantae subsp. subcordata, also known as the New Mexico fern, is a type of fern native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to treat skin conditions such as wounds and burns. The health benefits of Notholaena marantae subsp. subcordata include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions.
